01

Introduction

Hi…! My name is Abeydeera Liyanage Dilumi Gimansha Karunarathna, and I am truly delighted to introduce myself to you. I am originally from the vibrant coastal city of Matara, located in the Southern Province of Sri Lanka, a place that has shaped my values, determination, and love for learning from a very young age.

My academic journey began at Sujatha Vidyalaya, Matara, a school that laid a strong foundation for both my personal and intellectual development. The values of discipline, perseverance, and excellence that I cultivated during those formative years have continued to guide me throughout my life. Building on that foundation, I am currently a final-year undergraduate student at the Sri Lanka Institute of Information Technology (SLIIT), pursuing a Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Information Technology — a journey that has been both challenging and immensely rewarding.

Throughout my academic career, I have developed a strong foundation in software development, database management, and systems analysis. However, it is the field of Quality Assurance that has truly captured my passion and professional ambition. I am deeply fascinated by the discipline of ensuring that software systems perform reliably, efficiently, and to the highest possible standard. I firmly believe that quality is not simply a phase in the software development lifecycle — it is a commitment and a mindset that must be woven into every stage of the process.

My interest in Quality Assurance stems from my naturally detail-oriented and analytical approach to problem-solving. I take great pride in my ability to examine systems critically, identify inconsistencies, and contribute to the delivery of solutions that genuinely meet user expectations. I have developed familiarity with key QA concepts including manual testing, test case design, defect tracking, and software quality standards, and I am eager to deepen my expertise in test automation and performance testing as I step into the professional world.

As I approach the completion of my undergraduate studies, I am enthusiastic and well-prepared to embark on a career as a Quality Assurance Engineer. I aspire to join a forward-thinking organisation where I can apply my technical knowledge, contribute to the delivery of high-quality software products, and continuously grow as a professional.I am committed to staying current with evolving industry practices and embracing every opportunity to improve both myself and the teams I work with.

Beyond my academic pursuits, I am a motivated and curious individual who values continuous self-improvement. I believe in maintaining a healthy balance between professional ambition and personal well-being, and I carry with me the resilience and work ethic that my upbringing in Matara and my years at Sujatha Vidyalaya instilled in me.

I am confident that the skills, knowledge, and values I have gained throughout this journey will allow me to make a meaningful and lasting contribution to the field of Quality Assurance, and I look forward to the exciting opportunities that lie ahead.

02

Reflective Journals

01
Business Writing

Throughout my academic life, I believed writing was simply about conveying information. However, the Business Writing session changed my perspective on professional communication. I learned that effective business writing requires clarity, conciseness, formality, and a strong awareness of the audience. One important lesson was the principle of conciseness. I realised that I often used unnecessarily long phrases when shorter and clearer alternatives could communicate the same idea more effectively.

I also found the guidance on formal language and inclusivity very valuable. Learning to avoid contractions, reduce passive voice, and replace informal words with more precise terms helped improve the quality of my writing. As a future Quality Assurance Engineer, I will need to write defect reports, test cases, and professional emails. Therefore, clear and professional writing is an essential skill that I will continue to develop throughout my career.

02
Presentation Skills

Before this session, presentations often made me feel anxious because I lacked a clear method for organising and delivering them confidently. The material on 52 phrases for better flowing English presentations helped me understand the importance of "signposting." I learned that linking phrases act as a structure that guides the audience smoothly through the presentation. This showed me that a good presentation is not only about the content but also about how clearly the speaker moves from one idea to another.

I also found it useful to learn phrases for each stage of a presentation, including the introduction, transitions, and conclusion. The advice to memorise the introduction helped me understand how a strong start can build confidence and create a positive first impression. As a future Quality Assurance professional, I will need to present test results and reports to different audiences, so developing clear and confident presentation skills will be essential for my career.

03
Report Writing

This session introduced me to the formal structure of report writing and helped me understand how reports differ from essays. Previously, I tended to write reports in a similar style to essays, focusing on continuous arguments rather than structured information. However, I learned that reports present information in clearly organised sections, often using headings, numbering, and bullet points so readers can quickly understand key points and findings.

I also learned about the main sections of a formal report, such as the title page, executive summary, discussion, conclusion, and recommendations. The idea that the executive summary is written last but appears first was particularly interesting because it ensures the summary accurately reflects the entire report. Additionally, the concept of SMART recommendations — specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, and timely — highlighted the importance of providing clear and practical suggestions. This session helped me understand that effective report writing requires structure, clarity, and a focus on delivering useful information.

04
Formal and Informal Letters

This session provided a clear understanding of different types of letter writing, including complaints, apologies, invitations, applications, and requests. What I found most valuable was learning how tone, structure, and language change depending on the situation and the relationship between the writer and the reader. I also learned the difference between formal and informal letters. Formal letters require proper structure, two addresses, full names, and appropriate closings such as "Yours faithfully" or "Yours sincerely," while informal letters allow a more relaxed and conversational style.

The section on application letters was especially useful for me as a final-year student preparing to enter the job market. I learned how to present my qualifications and experience in a clear and professional way. The lesson on complaint letters also showed the importance of maintaining a polite and constructive tone, even when expressing dissatisfaction. Overall, this session helped me understand that effective letter writing is an important professional communication skill.

05
Memo Writing

Before this session, I had limited knowledge about memos beyond knowing they were used for internal workplace communication. This topic helped me understand that a memo, short for memorandum, is a formal document used within an organisation to share important information, address issues, or request action. I learned that memos should be clear, direct, and focused on a single purpose so that readers can quickly understand the message.

I also found the eight-step process for writing a memo very helpful, especially the structure that includes the heading, opening statement, context, discussion, and call to action. The examples provided made it easier to see how memos are used in real workplace situations. As a future Quality Assurance Engineer, I may need to send memos to teams about testing schedules, defects, or process changes, so this session helped me develop the skills to write clear and professional internal communications.

06
Professional Email Writing

This session helped me understand how to write professional emails clearly and effectively. I learned that email is one of the most common forms of communication in the workplace, so it is important to use it properly. One key lesson was the importance of a clear subject line, which should briefly explain the purpose of the email and help the recipient understand its priority. I also learned the correct use of CC and BCC fields and how they should be used carefully in professional communication.

Another important aspect was maintaining the correct tone in emails. Using appropriate greetings such as "Dear," "Hello," or "Hi" depending on the relationship with the recipient helps maintain professionalism. I also learned to avoid slang, emojis, and overly informal language in business emails. As a future Quality Assurance professional, I will communicate regularly with developers, testers, and managers through email, so writing clear, polite, and professional messages will be an essential skill in my career.

03

Career Development Plan

Name:Abeydeera Liyanage Dilumi Gimansha Karunarathna
Current Status:Final-year undergraduate, BSc (Hons) in Information Technology, SLIIT
Career Goal:Quality Assurance Engineer
Vision:To become a highly skilled and respected QA professional who contributes to the delivery of reliable, high-quality software products, and to eventually grow into a senior leadership role within the quality assurance domain.

Personal SWOT Analysis

Strengths

  • Strong analytical & detail-oriented thinking
  • Solid IT academic foundation at SLIIT
  • Good written & verbal communication (PPW)
  • Self-motivated, committed to continuous learning
  • Effective in independent and team settings

Weaknesses

  • Limited hands-on formal QA industry experience
  • Test automation tools knowledge needs strengthening
  • Limited practical Agile/DevOps exposure
  • Public speaking confidence still developing

Opportunities

  • Growing demand for QA Engineers locally & globally
  • Wide availability of online QA certifications
  • SLIIT alumni network & industry connections
  • Remote work opens international opportunities

Threats

  • Competitive job market for entry-level IT roles
  • Rapid technology evolution requires constant upskilling
  • AI-driven automated testing tools require adaptation
Short-Term Goals (0 – 1 Year)

Goal 1 — Graduate with Honours

  • Successfully complete the final year at SLIIT, submitting all assessments to a high standard.
  • Perform well in final examinations to achieve an honours-level result.
  • Strengthen the professional profile and demonstrate a commitment to academic excellence.

Goal 2 — Obtain a Recognised QA Certification

  • Pursue and complete the ISTQB Foundation Level Certificate in Software Testing.
  • Validate theoretical knowledge of testing principles and test design techniques.
  • Gain a globally recognised qualification highly valued by employers in the software industry.

Goal 3 — Secure an Entry-Level QA Engineer Position

  • Actively apply for QA Engineer or Software Tester roles in Sri Lankan IT companies.
  • Target organisations that work with Agile methodologies.
  • Tailor CVs and cover letters using formal writing skills developed through PPW.
  • Prepare thoroughly for technical interviews.
Medium-Term Goals (1 – 3 Years)

Goal 5 — Gain Hands-On Experience in Test Automation

  • Move beyond manual testing and develop strong competency in automated testing frameworks.
  • Become proficient in Selenium WebDriver with Java or Python, and TestNG.
  • Build skills in Cypress for front-end testing.
  • Recognise automation as an increasingly essential skill in modern QA practice.

Goal 6 — Pursue ISTQB Advanced Level Certification

  • After gaining practical experience, pursue the ISTQB Advanced Level — Test Analyst certification.
  • Deepen technical expertise and demonstrate advanced competency to employers.

Goal 8 — Build a Professional Network

  • Actively engage with the Sri Lankan and international QA and IT professional community.
  • Connect through LinkedIn, industry meetups, and SLIIT alumni networks.
  • Open doors to new opportunities, mentorship, and industry insights.
Long-Term Goals (3 – 5+ Years)

Goal 9 — Progress to Senior QA Engineer or QA Lead Role

  • Accumulate 3–5 years of solid experience in both manual and automated testing.
  • Aim to be recognised as a senior contributor within a QA team.
  • Mentor junior testers and lead test planning activities.
  • Communicate quality metrics clearly to project stakeholders.

Goal 10 — Explore Specialisation in Performance or Security Testing

  • Explore performance testing using tools like JMeter.
  • Investigate security testing to address the growing importance of cybersecurity in software development.
  • Develop a niche specialisation to stand out in the QA field.

Goal 11 — Contribute to the QA Community

  • Mentor junior QA professionals entering the field.
  • Contribute to industry forums and blogs to share knowledge and experience.
  • Potentially conduct workshops or training sessions for aspiring QA engineers.

Curriculum Vitae

05

Evidence of a Skill Course

As part of my commitment to continuous personal and professional development, I have actively pursued both technical and soft skill development through recognised institutions and platforms over the past year. The following certifications reflect my dedication to expanding my knowledge beyond the classroom and equipping myself with the skills necessary to thrive as a Quality Assurance Engineer in a competitive and rapidly evolving industry.

Certificate 01

Diploma in English

Institution
Sanit Campus, Kandy
Completion
February 2026
Skill Type
Communication (Soft Skill)
Summary

This diploma improved my English language proficiency in writing, grammar, reading, and speaking. It strengthened my ability to communicate clearly and professionally.

Career Relevance

As a Quality Assurance Engineer, strong communication is essential for writing defect reports, test cases, and professional emails. This qualification helps me communicate technical information clearly in a professional environment.

Certificate 02

Higher Diploma in Information Technology

Institution
SLIIT, Malabe
Completion
December 2024
Skill Type
Information Technology
Summary

This programme provided knowledge in software development, databases, networking, systems analysis, and web technologies.

Career Relevance

It strengthened my technical foundation, helping me understand software systems and collaborate effectively with developers and project teams during testing processes.

Certificate 03

AWS Cloud Web Application Builder Training

Institution
Amazon Web Services (AWS)
Completion
September 2025
Skill Type
Cloud Computing
Summary

This training introduced cloud concepts and the process of building and deploying web applications using AWS services.

Career Relevance

It improved my understanding of cloud-based environments, enabling me to test modern applications hosted in cloud infrastructure.

Certificate 04

AWS SimuLearn: Highly Available Web Application

Institution
Amazon Web Services (AWS)
Completion
September 2025
Skill Type
Cloud Architecture & Reliability
Summary

This simulation-based training focused on building reliable applications using load balancing, auto-scaling, and fault tolerance.

Career Relevance

It enhanced my ability to design QA test scenarios related to system reliability, performance, and availability.

Certificate 05

AWS SimuLearn: Database in Practice

Institution
Amazon Web Services (AWS)
Completion
September 2025
Skill Type
Database & Cloud
Summary

This course covered database management, configuration, and performance optimisation within AWS environments.

Career Relevance

It strengthened my skills in database testing, ensuring data accuracy, integrity, and performance in software systems.

Reflection

These certifications represent my commitment to continuous learning. They combine communication skills with technical knowledge in IT and cloud computing, helping me develop the competencies required for a successful career in Quality Assurance Engineering.
